payload.description: Records the procurement details, pathological characteristics, and storage information of biological samples obtained from patients during clinical events.
Unique identifier assigned to the patient (e.g., TCGA barcode prefix).
Number of days from a baseline index date (like diagnosis) to the sample acquisition event.
Number of days from a baseline index date to the end of the acquisition event, if applicable.
Categorization of the specific timeline event occurring.
Unique code assigned to the specific biospecimen acquired.
The surgical or clinical procedure used to obtain the sample.
The country where the sample acquisition took place.
Indicator of whether the sample underwent a preliminary evaluation before inclusion.
Indicates if the top section slide of the tissue block was submitted for review.
Estimated percentage of the sample area showing tissue death/necrosis.
Estimated percentage of total nuclei in the sample that belong to tumor cells.
Measured weight of the acquired tumor sample, usually in milligrams.
The type of container or medium used to store the biospecimen.
